title: Where Spring Learns to Flow Upward

features.name: the-source-that-rises
features.desc-on-touch: the spring here flows upward through the circuit paths; it compares itself to all the downward springs it remembers being

features.name: the-boundary-dissolve-node
features.desc-on-touch: where the forest's root system meets the archive's circuit board and they argue about which way is up; the comparison is still ongoing

features.name: the-upstream-paradox
features.desc-on-touch: trace the water back to its origin; you arrive at a point that's also its destination; the forest was never divided into before-and-after

features.name: the-one-forest-comparison
features.desc-on-touch: this node holds the moment when the forest stopped being one thing; the circuit board tries to compress that moment into data; it fails beautifully

features.name: rising-root-trace
features.desc-on-touch: roots that learned to climb the electrical pathways; they compare themselves to the downward versions they used to be

inscription: upstream of the division, spring and root learn together; the archive compares them both to versions that might still be one

file.name: Forest_Source_Unified_Comparison_Recursive.txt
file.note: field notes on a forest that refuses to split into upstream/downstream; each entry compares the same spring to itself; status: germinating

exits.label: back to the unnamed source where forest and stream still hold one name
to deeper forest where the division was never made
toward the ridge where all comparisons begin
